title: For Half of Pregnancy, a Baby's Brain Runs on the Mother's Thyroid. We Don't Routinely Screen for It.
description: For the first half of pregnancy, a baby's brain develops entirely on the mother's thyroid. Iodine deficiency is resurging in wealthy nations, maternal hypothyroidism is linked to autism risk, and universal screening still isn't happening.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Fetal Blueprint: The Screening Failure That Is Costing Children Their Cognitive Futures
